objective: Close red-team gaps in auto thread-on-invoke routing, task continuation, and operator diagnostics
scope_summary: Closed the remaining auto thread-on-invoke gaps in foreground and worker response-surface recovery, threaded task continuation, and governance/operator health reporting.
constraints: Keep the fix limited to the invoke-thread routing seams rather than reopening the broader Discord operations rewrite.
Preserve the explicit originChannelId/responseChannelId model introduced by the thread-on-invoke feature.

changes.summary: Added fail-open recovery when a stored response channel or thread can no longer be fetched, so waiting and stale-completed continuations fall back to the current channel instead of dropping the turn.

Allowed waiting and running task matching by sourceMessageId for threaded response surfaces, so parent-channel replies to the original source message resume the existing task instead of forking a new one.

Made worker-side response publishing fail open to the origin channel when a threaded response surface disappears, and treated that fallback as a deliberate response-surface reset so stale canonical metadata does not block recovery.
Restricted persisted thread reuse to running and waiting-user-input task runs so approval-paused threads no longer attract fresh parent-channel invokes.
Added invoke-thread permission health inspection to governance status and rejected enablement when Sage lacks CreatePublicThreads or SendMessagesInThreads.
Added regressions for missing threaded response-surface fallback and parent-channel continuation into the existing thread.
Added matcher coverage for threaded source-message replies on waiting and running task runs.
Added regression coverage for worker-side origin-channel fallback when a stored response thread disappears.
Added router coverage to ensure source-message thread reuse only targets running and waiting-user-input task runs.
Added governance coverage for invoke-thread permission diagnostics and broken-policy rejection.
Documented the operator-visible impact of the invoke-thread hardening.

validations.notes: Artifact schemas validated after the plan/report updates.
Full Vitest suite passed: 138 files, 833 tests.
Markdown lint and tracked doc-link checks passed.

validations.command: npm run test:trust + npm run check:mutation
validations.notes: Completed the deep trust components individually after the combined wrapper exceeded tool timeouts; repeated trust runs, tool audit, and mutation-critical coverage all passed.

evidence: Targeted Vitest regressions for messageCreate ingest routing, worker publish recovery, invoke-thread router status filtering, agentTaskRunRepo matching, and governance wrapper diagnostics passed before the full suite.
Final end-to-end review of the updated invoke-thread routing, task continuation, worker recovery, and governance paths found no new in-scope issues.
decisions: Fail open to the current channel when a persisted response thread disappears, rather than treating that as a fatal continuation error.
Only treat parent-message replies as task continuations when the task's response surface is a distinct thread, so same-channel ambiguous resumes stay blocked.
Require current bot thread permissions when enabling invoke-thread routing and surface the same health through governance status.
Treat worker fallback to the origin channel as a response-surface reset so stale canonical-response metadata cannot suppress the recovered reply.
Exclude waiting-approval task runs from persisted thread reuse so fresh parent-channel invokes do not get rerouted into approval-paused threads.
risks: The broader Discord thread-on-invoke rewrite is still uncommitted local work in this branch; this report only covers the final hardening delta.
